codeReview.summary: Implementation correctly fulfills the feature specification. Promotion thresholds are properly implemented: Patterns require ≥5 confirmations with confidence >0.6; Rules (Conventions) require ≥10 confirmations with confidence >0.8. Boundary conditions correctly use > (not ≥) for confidence comparisons. The 1.5x retrieval rank boost is implemented via get_retrieval_rank_boost() method.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The get_retrieval_rank_boost() method is implemented and tested, but the recall module (recall.rs) does not use AntiPattern or Convention for retrieval - it uses conversation logs. The 'higher retrieval rank' behavior described in expectedBehavior is not yet integrated into the actual retrieval flow.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: grep 'AntiPattern|Convention' recall.rs returns no matches. get_retrieval_rank_boost only appears in pattern_learning.rs tests and implementation. This is a known integration gap - the promotion mechanism exists but is not yet wired into recall.

summary: Feature implementation passes review. Code correctly implements pattern promotion (≥5 conf/>0.6) and rule promotion (≥10 conf/>0.8) with boundary conditions handled correctly (confidence uses strict > comparison). All 15 promotion tests pass. Non-blocking observation: retrieval integration not yet complete - the boost method exists but recall.rs doesn't use AntiPattern/Convention for ranking.
